Why this recommendation?
Recommendations are built from machining evidence: material match, operation fit, geometry logic, tradeoffs and confidence quality.
Material match: ISO P/M/K/N/S/H fit is checked against each product's material window and cutting data.
Operation fit: turning, milling, drilling, threading and grooving are matched to declared operations and family signals.
Geometry logic: chipbreaker, edge type, coating and series/family cues help separate stable fits from specialist options.
Tradeoffs: the card calls out when a tool favors tool life, productivity, edge security, value or a niche use case.
Confidence: the score reflects mapping strength and comparable data quality. It is decision support, not a guaranteed machining result.

3
Step 3 — Machining Conditions
Define the cutting conditions for optimal tool selection.
🔩
Long tool life
Maximize inserts between tool changes. Stability and wear resistance first.
⚡
High productivity
Maximum metal removal rate. Higher Vc and feed, accepting shorter tool life.
✨
Surface finish
Ra requirement is the priority. Low feed, wiper geometry, positive rake.
🏔️
Difficult conditions
Interrupted cuts, vibration, unstable setup, hard-to-machine materials.

Vc/fn quick reference
P: 180–400 m/min · M: 80–220 · K: 150–400 · N: 300–1500 · S: 20–70 · H: 60–220. Use lower end for unstable setups.

AI Chat

Process Q&A for cutting tool decisions

Cutting Tool Beta Guide Local assistant
Ask me anything about cutting tools, ISO codes, grades, coatings or material selection. Add your Anthropic API key in ⚙ Settings to enable live Claude responses.
Best insert for Inconel 718?
Inconel 718 (ISO S):

• Sandvik GC2220 — PVD TiAlN, best for turning S05–S20
• ISCAR IC806 — SUMO TEC®, great for interrupted cuts

Vc = 25–45 m/min · High-pressure coolant mandatory · Consistent DOC to avoid work hardening.

Optional technical resources

Secondary layer only: guides, references and optional consultation
Technical glossary
ISO material group
P steel, M stainless, K cast iron, N non-ferrous, S superalloy/titanium, H hardened steel.
Chipbreaker
Insert edge geometry that controls chip shape, cutting force and stability.
CVD / PVD coatings
CVD favors wear resistance and stable steel cuts; PVD favors sharp edges and lower cutting force.
TiAlN / TiCN / Al2O3
Common coating layers for heat resistance, toughness, wear resistance and crater protection.
Vc / fn / ap
Cutting speed, feed per revolution/tooth, and depth of cut.
MRR / kc1
Material removal rate and specific cutting force used for power estimation.
Nose radius
Corner radius controlling edge strength, finish potential and profile accuracy.
Rake / clearance angle
Rake changes cutting force; clearance prevents rubbing behind the edge.
Edge prep / hone / T-land
Micro-edge preparation that trades sharpness for edge strength and reliability.
Wiper geometry
Modified corner geometry that improves finish at higher feed rates when setup is stable.
Coating types
TiN — yellow general-purpose coating.
TiCN — gray-blue wear resistance.
TiAlN — purple-violet heat resistance.
AlTiN — anthracite high-temp coating.
Al2O3 — black alumina wear layer.
DLC — matte black low-friction layer.

Platform reference
Data sources
ToolAdvisor references cutting tool brand catalogues, ISO material group logic and public machining handbook conventions. Always validate with manufacturer data before production.
Methodology
Recommendations combine operation, material group, coating, geometry, family, cross-reference and confidence signals.
Brand neutrality
Independent Ranking is brand-neutral and technical-fit driven; no brand receives a hidden priority boost.
How to contribute
Suggest products or report errors from product cards, or email support@tooladvisor.eu with source references.

🔄 Turning Inserts
📐
ISO P — Steel
Steel Turning Complete Grade Guide
All major brand grades for P01–P50. Vc/fn/ap tables, coating guide, when to use what.

ISO M — Stainless
Stainless Steel Machining Handbook
316L, 304, duplex, PH grades. Work hardening prevention, coolant, recommended inserts.
🚀
ISO S — Superalloys
Inconel & Titanium Machining Guide
Ti-6Al-4V, Inconel 718, Hastelloy. Cutting data, coolant pressure, insert grade selection.

Why Trust This Catalogue
Each card exposes confidence and why-it-fits context so you can validate recommendations before buying or comparing.
Confidence meaning: signal coverage across material, operation, geometry, family and comparable alternatives.
Why this result: concise fit statement derived from active signals and current filter context.
Warnings: low-confidence and limited-comparable-data labels are shown explicitly when evidence is weak.
Drag two product cards here for quick compare.
Last viewed products

Carbide insert cross-reference FAQ

What is the Sandvik equivalent of IC8250? ToolAdvisor helps CNC machinists compare ISCAR IC8250 with Sandvik, Kennametal, Walter, Tungaloy and other carbide insert grades by ISO material group, operation, coating and application fit.

How do I cross-reference carbide inserts? Search an insert code such as CNMG120408, WNMG, DCMT, APMT or SNMG, then compare equivalent carbide insert grades across major cutting tool brands using operation and material context.

What grade should I use for stainless steel turning? For ISO M stainless steel turning, compare PVD and stainless-focused carbide grades by stability, coating type, chipbreaker geometry and recommended cutting data before choosing a production insert.

Carbide insert cross-reference tool for CNC machinists. Compare ISO P, M, K, N, S, H material group inserts. Find equivalents for CNMG, WNMG, DCMT, APMT, SNMG insert codes across all major brands.
